briefdescription.para: Save current Tensor to file.

detaileddescription.para:
fname
file name (without file extension)
save the Tensor to file with file path specify with input param fname with postfix ".cytn"
Load(const std::string &fname)

briefdescription.para: Save current Tensor to the binary file.

detaileddescription.para:
This function will save the Tensor to the binary file with file name fname .
fname[in]
the file name of the binary file.
The file name fname must be valid.
cytnx::Tensor::Fromfile

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< cytnx_uint64 > &shape, unsigned int dtype=Type.Double, int device=-1, bool init_zero=true)
memberdef.name: Init

briefdescription.para: initialize a Tensor

parameterdescription.para: the dtype of tensor. This can be any of type defined in cytnx::Type
the device that tensor to be created. This can be cytnx::Device.cpu or
if true, the content of Tensor will be initialized to zero. if false, the content of Tensor will be un-initialize. cytnx::Device.cuda+<gpuid>, see cytnx::Device for more detail.

simplesect.para: The content of Tensor created will be un-initialize! See zeros(), ones() or arange() for generating an Tensor.

memberdef.argsstring: (std::initializer_list< cytnx_uint64 > shape, unsigned int dtype=Type.Double, int device=-1, bool init_zero=true)
memberdef.name: Init

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< cytnx_uint64 > &shape, unsigned int dtype=Type.Double, int device=-1, bool init_zero=true)
memberdef.name: Tensor

briefdescription.para: Construct a new Tensor object.

detaileddescription.para:
This is the constructor of Tensor. It will call cytnx::Tensor::Init() to initialize the Tensor.
shape
the shape of tensor
dtype
the dtype of tensor. This can be any of type defined in cytnx::Type.
device
the device that tensor to be created. This can be cytnx::Device.cpu or cytnx::Device.cuda+<gpuid>, see cytnx::Device for more detail.
init_zero
if true, the content of Tensor will be initialized to zero. If false, the content of Tensor will be un-initialized.
cytnx::Tensor::Init

memberdef.argsstring: (std::initializer_list< cytnx_uint64 > shape, unsigned int dtype=Type.Double, int device=-1, bool init_zero=true)
memberdef.name: Tensor

briefdescription.para: the dtype-id of the Tensor

simplesect.para: cytnx::Type
[unsigned int] the dtype_id of the Tensor

briefdescription.para: the device-id of the Tensor

simplesect.para: [int] the device_id of the Tensor

briefdescription.para: the dtype (in string) of the Tensor

simplesect.para: cytnx::Type, dtype() const
[std::string] the dtype of the Tensor

briefdescription.para: the device (in string) of the Tensor

simplesect.para: cytnx::Device, device() const
[std::string] the device of the Tensor

briefdescription.para: the shape of the Tensor

simplesect.para: [std::vector<cytnx_uint64>] the shape of the Tensor

briefdescription.para: the storage strides of the Tensor

detaileddescription.para:
[std::vector<cytnx_int64>] for each logical axis, the distance in the underlying storage between consecutive elements along that axis
cytnx tensors store a dense permutation of their logical axes, so the stride of every axis is well defined (this is the layout Tensor::at indexes through). For a contiguous tensor these are the row-major strides; for a permuted (non-contiguous) view they reflect the permuted memory order.

briefdescription.para: the rank of the Tensor

simplesect.para: [cytnx_uint64] the rank of the Tensor

briefdescription.para: Return the total number of logical elements in the Tensor.

simplesect.para: [cytnx_uint64] 1 for a rank-0 scalar and 0 for a Tensor with any zero extent.

briefdescription.para: whether the Tensor is uninitialized.

simplesect.para: [bool] true if the Tensor has Type.Void dtype.

briefdescription.para: whether the Tensor is an initialized rank-0 scalar Tensor

simplesect.para: [bool] true if the Tensor has rank 0 and a non-void dtype

briefdescription.para: whether the Tensor is initialized and has no elements

simplesect.para: [bool] true if the Tensor has a non-void dtype and size() == 0

briefdescription.para: return a clone of the current Tensor.

detaileddescription.para:
[Tensor]
In C++ API, the behavior of assignment operator is designed to have same behavior as python, to have a copy of the current tensor, we call clone to return a copy.

briefdescription.para: copy a tensor to new device

detaileddescription.para:
device
the device-id that is moving to. it can be any device defined in cytnx::Device
[Tensor]
description: if the device-id is the same as current Tensor's device, then return self. otherwise, return a copy of instance that located on the target device. see also: Tensor.to_ Example:

para.verbatim: cytnx device: CUDA/GPU-id:0 cytnx device: CPU
para.heading: python API:

para.verbatim: cytnx device: CUDA/GPU-id:0 cytnx device: CPU

briefdescription.para: move the current Tensor to the device.

detaileddescription.para:
device
the device-id that is moving to. it can be any device defined in cytnx::Device
description: see also: Tensor.to Example:

briefdescription.para: return whether the Tensor is contiguous or not.

simplesect.para: [bool] true if the Tensor is contiguous, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: perform tensor permute on the cytnx::Tensor and return a new instance.

parameterdescription.para: the permute indices, should have No. of elements equal to the rank of tensor.

simplesect.para: [Tensor] a permuted new Tensor

listitem.para: The size of input and output Tensor should be the same.

para.verbatim:
Vector Print: Total Elements:3 [3, 4, 5]
Vector Print: Total Elements:3 [3, 5, 4]
0 1
para.heading: python API:

briefdescription.para: Make the Tensor contiguous by coalescing the memory (storage).

simplesect.para: [Tensor] a new Tensor that is with contiguous memory (storage).
If the input tensor is not contiguous, then the data will be stored in new memory; otherwise, the input tensor is returned and no cloning of the data happens.

para.verbatim:
Vector Print: Total Elements:3 [3, 4, 5]
Vector Print: Total Elements:3 [3, 5, 4]
0 1 Vector Print: Total Elements:3 [3, 5, 4]
0 1
para.heading: python API:

briefdescription.para: Make the Tensor contiguous by coalescing the memory (storage), inplacely.

para.verbatim:
Vector Print: Total Elements:3 [3, 4, 5]
Vector Print: Total Elements:3 [3, 5, 4]
0 1
para.heading: python API:

briefdescription.para: reshape the Tensor, inplacely

parameterdescription.para: the new shape of the Tensor.

listitem.para: The size of input and output Tensor should be the same.

simplesect.para: Compare to reshape(), this function will not create a new Tensor, but reshape the current Tensor inplacely.

briefdescription.para: return a new Tensor that is reshaped.

parameterdescription.para: the new shape of the Tensor.

simplesect.para: [Tensor]

listitem.para: The size of input and output Tensor should be the same.
This function will not change the original Tensor.
You can use Tensor::reshape_() to reshape the Tensor inplacely.
You can set new_shape to -1, which will be automatically determined by the size of the Tensor. The behavior is the same as numpy.reshape().

briefdescription.para: return a new Tensor that cast to different dtype.

parameterdescription.para: the new dtype. It can be any type defined in cytnx::Type

simplesect.para: [Tensor]
If the new_type is the same as dtype of the current Tensor, return self.
This function cannot convert complex type to real type, please use Tensor::real() or Tensor::imag() to get the real or imaginary part of the complex Tensor instead.

briefdescription.para: Get an element at specific location.

detaileddescription.para:
This function is used to get an element at specific location. If the template type is not given, the return will be a Scalar.
locator
the location of the element
[ref]
This is for C++ API only!
need template instantiation to resolve the type, which should be consist with the dtype of the Tensor. An error will be issued if the template type is inconsist with the current dtype of Tensor.
For python API, use [] directly to get element.
Example:

briefdescription.para: get the element from a rank-0 Tensor.

detaileddescription.para:
This function is used to get the element from a rank-0 Tensor. If the template type is not given, the return will be a Scalar.
[T]
This can only be called on a rank-0 Tensor (scalar). For C++ API, a template instantiation of type is needed to resolve the type, which should be connsist with the dtype of the Tensor. An error will be issued if the template type if inconsist with the current dtype of Tensor.
Although the return is by reference in C++ part, the return in python is not.
From 2., We recommend user to use at<T> (C++ API) and [] (python API) to modify the value of the element to have consistant syntax across two languages.
Example:

para.verbatim:
Total elem: 1 type : Uint64 cytnx device: CPU Shape : (1) [ 1 ]
1
para.heading: python API:

para.verbatim:
Total elem: 1 type : Uint64 cytnx device: CPU Shape : (1) [ 1 ]
1

briefdescription.para: get elements using Accessor (C++ API) / slices (python API)

parameterdescription.para: the Accessor (C++ API) / slices (python API) to get the elements.
the indices that were removed from the original shape of the Tensor are pushed to the end of this vector. Usually, an empty vector should be passed.

simplesect.para: [Tensor]
Accessor for cordinate with Accessor in C++ API.
The return will be a new Tensor instance, which does not share memory with the current Tensor.

detaileddescription.para: One can also using more intruisive way to get the slice using [] operator.

briefdescription.para: set elements with the input Tensor using Accessor (C++ API) / slices (python API)

simplesect.para: : the shape of the input Tensor should be the same as the shape that indicated using Accessor. The memory is not shared with the input Tensor.

briefdescription.para: set elements with the input constant using Accessor (C++ API) / slices (python API)

simplesect.para: Tensor::fill for filling all elements with assigned constant.

briefdescription.para: return the storage of current Tensor.

simplesect.para: [Storage]
The return storage shares the same instance of the storage of current Tensor. Use Storage.clone() to create a new instance of the returned Storage.

briefdescription.para: fill all the element of current Tensor with the value.
compare the shape of two tensors.
return the real part of the tensor.

simplesect.para: [Tensor] the real part of the tensor.
the tensor must be complex type (Type.ComplexDouble or Type.ComplexFloat).
cytnx::Type

briefdescription.para: return the imaginary part of the tensor.

simplesect.para: [Tensor] the imaginary part of the tensor.
the tensor must be complex type (Type.ComplexDouble or Type.ComplexFloat).
cytnx::Type

briefdescription.para: addition assignment operator with a Tensor or a scalar.

detaileddescription.para:
This function will add the template type to the current tensor, inplacely. The template can be either a scalar or a tensor. If the template is a scalar, then the scalar will be added to all the elements of the current tensor. If the template is a tensor, then the shape of the template tensor must be the same as the current tensor. The supported type of the template are Tensor, Scalar or any scalar type (see cytnx_complex128, cytnx_complex64, cytnx_double, cytnx_float, cytnx_int64, cytnx_int32, cytnx_int16, cytnx_uint64, cytnx_uint32, cytnx_uint16, cytnx_bool).
rc
the added Tensor or scalar.
If the template type is Tensor, then the shape of the template tensor must be the same as the current tensor.

briefdescription.para: subtraction assignment operator with a Tensor or a scalar.

detaileddescription.para:
This function will subtract the template type to the current tensor, inplacely. The template can be either a scalar or a tensor. If the template is a scalar, then the scalar will be subtracted to all the elements of the current tensor. If the template is a tensor, then the shape of the template tensor must be the same as the current tensor. The supported type of the template are Tensor, Scalar or any scalar type (see cytnx_complex128, cytnx_complex64, cytnx_double, cytnx_float, cytnx_int64, cytnx_int32, cytnx_int16, cytnx_uint64, cytnx_uint32, cytnx_uint16, cytnx_bool).
rc
the subtracted Tensor or scalar.
If the template type is Tensor, then the shape of the template tensor must be the same as the current tensor.

briefdescription.para: multiplication assignment operator with a Tensor or a scalar.

detaileddescription.para:
This function will multiply the template type to the current tensor, inplacely. The template can be either a scalar or a tensor. If the template is a scalar, then the scalar will be multiplied to all the elements of the current tensor. If the template is a tensor, then the shape of the template tensor must be the same as the current tensor. The supported type of the template are Tensor, Scalar or any scalar type (see cytnx_complex128, cytnx_complex64, cytnx_double, cytnx_float, cytnx_int64, cytnx_int32, cytnx_int16, cytnx_uint64, cytnx_uint32, cytnx_uint16, cytnx_bool).
rc
the multiplied Tensor or scalar.
If the template type is Tensor, then the shape of the template tensor must be the same as the current tensor.

briefdescription.para: division assignment operator with a Tensor or a scalar.

detaileddescription.para:
This function will divide the template type to the current tensor, inplacely. The template can be either a scalar or a tensor. If the template is a scalar, then the scalar will be divided to all the elements of the current tensor. If the template is a tensor, then the shape of the template tensor must be the same as the current tensor. The supported type of the template are Tensor, Scalar or any scalar type (see cytnx_complex128, cytnx_complex64, cytnx_double, cytnx_float, cytnx_int64, cytnx_int32, cytnx_int16, cytnx_uint64, cytnx_uint32, cytnx_uint16, cytnx_bool).
rc
the divided Tensor or scalar.
If the template type is Tensor, then the shape of the template tensor must be the same as the current tensor.
rc cannot be zero.

briefdescription.para: Addition function with a Tensor or a scalar. Same as cytnx::operator+(const Tensor &self, const T &rhs).
Addition function with a Tensor or a scalar, inplacely. Same as operator+=(const T &rhs).
Subtraction function with a Tensor or a scalar. Same as cytnx::operator-(const Tensor &self, const T &rhs).
Subtraction function with a Tensor or a scalar, inplacely. Same as operator-=(const T &rhs).
Multiplication function with a Tensor or a scalar. Same as cytnx::operator*(const Tensor &self, const T &rhs).
Multiplication function with a Tensor or a scalar, inplacely. Same as operator*=(const T &rhs).
Division function with a Tensor or a scalar. Same as cytnx::operator/(const Tensor &self, const T &rhs).

parameterdescription.para: the divided Tensor or scalar. @attension rhs cannot be zero.

briefdescription.para: Division function with a Tensor or a scalar, inplacely. Same as operator/=(const T &rhs).

parameterdescription.para: the divided Tensor or scalar. @attension rhs cannot be zero.

briefdescription.para: The comparison function.

detaileddescription.para:
This function is the comparison function. Same as cytnx::operator==(const Tensor &self, const T &rhs).
rhs
the compared object.

briefdescription.para: The negation function.

detaileddescription.para:
This function is the negation function. Namely, if the current tensor is $A$, then the output tensor is $-A$.
The negation of the current tensor.

briefdescription.para: The flatten function.

detaileddescription.para:
This function is the flatten function. It will clone (deep copy) , contiguos the current tensor and reshape it to 1-rank Tensor.
compare to the flatten_() function, this function will return a new tensor and the current tensor will not be changed.

briefdescription.para: The flatten function, inplacely.

detaileddescription.para:
This function is the flatten function, inplacely. It will contiguos the current tensor and reshape it to 1-rank Tensor.
compare to the flatten() function, this is an inplacely function, the current tensor will be changed.

briefdescription.para: the append function.

detaileddescription.para:
This function is the append function. It will append the rhs tensor to the current tensor. The rhs tensor must have the same shape as the current tensor, except the first dimension. For example, if the current tensor is $A(i,j,k)$ and the rhs tensor is $B(j,k)$, then the output tensor is $C(i,j,k)$ where \[ C(i,j,k) = \begin{cases} A(i,j,k) & \text{if } i \neq N \\ B(j,k) & \text{if } i = N \end{cases} \] where $N$ is the number of the first dimension of the current tensor. Here indices $i$, $j$ and $k$ start from 0.
rhs
the appended tensor.
The appended tensor.
The rhs tensor and the current tensor cannot be empty.
The rhs tensor must have the same shape as the current tensor, except the first dimension. Namely, rhs.shape()[i] == this->shape()[i+1] and rhs.shape().size() == this->shape().size()-1.
If the dtype of the rhs is different from the current tensor, the rhs will be casted to the dtype of the current tensor.
append(const Storage &rhs)

briefdescription.para: the append function of the Storage.

detaileddescription.para:
This function is the append function of the Storage. It will append the srhs Storage to the current tensor. The current tensor must be rank-2 and the srhs Storage must have the same size as the second dimension of the current tensor. For example, if the current tensor is $A$ with size $M \times N$ and the srhs Storage is $B$ with size $N$, then the output tensor is $C$ with size $M \times (N+1)$ where \[ C(i,j) = \begin{cases} A(i,j) & \text{if } j \neq N \\ B(i) & \text{if } j = N \end{cases} \] Here indices $i$ and $j$ start from 0.
srhs
the appended Storage.
The appended tensor.
The srhs Storage and the current tensor cannot be empty.
The current tensor must be rank-2.
The srhs Storage must have the same size as the second dimension of the current tensor. Namely, srhs.size() == this->shape()[1].
If the dtype of the srhs is different from the current tensor, the srhs will be casted to the dtype of the current tensor.
append(const Tensor &rhs)

briefdescription.para: the append function of the scalar.

detaileddescription.para:
This function is the append function of the scalar. It can only append scalar into rank-1 Tensor.
rhs
the appended scalar.
The appended tensor.
The current Tensor must be rank-1. (1D array)
The current Tensor must be contiguous.
rhs must be a scalar.

briefdescription.para: Check whether two tensors share the same internal memory.

detaileddescription.para:
This function will check whether two tensors share the same internal memory. If the two tensors share the same internal memory, then the function will return true. Otherwise, it will return false. See user guide for more details.
rhs
the tensor to be compared.

briefdescription.para: the SVD member function. Same as cytnx::linalg::Svd(const Tensor &Tin, const bool &is_UvT) , where Tin is the current Tensor.
the Eigh member function. Same as cytnx::linalg::Eigh(const Tensor &Tin, const bool &is_V, const bool &row_v) , where Tin is the current Tensor.
the InvM_ member function. Same as cytnx::linalg::InvM_(Tensor &Tin), where Tin is the current Tensor.
the InvM member function. Same as cytnx::linalg::InvM(const Tensor &Tin), where Tin is the current Tensor.
Apply the inverse on each entry of the Tensor.

parameterdescription.para: elmements with absolute value <= clip are set to zero; corresponds to the pseudo-inverse

simplesect.para: Compared to Inv(), this function is an inplace function.
Inv(const double &clip)

briefdescription.para: Apply the inverse on each entry of the Tensor.

parameterdescription.para: elmements with absolute value <= clip are set to zero; corresponds to the pseudo-inverse

simplesect.para: Compared to Inv_(), this function will create a new Tensor.

briefdescription.para: the Conj_ member function. Same as cytnx::linalg::Conj_(Tensor &Tin), where Tin is the current Tensor.
the Conj member function. Same as cytnx::linalg::Conj(const Tensor &Tin), where Tin is the current Tensor.
the Exp_ member function. Same as linalg::Exp_(Tensor &Tin), where Tin is the current Tensor.
the Exp member function. Same as linalg::Exp(const Tensor &Tin), where Tin is the current Tensor.
the Norm member function. Same as linalg::Norm(const Tensor &Tin), where Tin is the current Tensor.
the Pow member function. Same as linalg::Pow(const Tensor &Tin, const cytnx_double &p), where Tin is the current Tensor.
the Pow_ member function. Same as linalg::Pow_(Tensor &Tin, const cytnx_double &p), where Tin is the current Tensor.
the Trace member function. Same as linalg::Trace(const Tensor &Tin, const cytnx_uint64 &a, const cytnx_uint64 &b), where Tin is the current Tensor.
the Abs member function. Same as linalg::Abs(const Tensor &Tin), where Tin is the current Tensor.
the Abs_ member function. Same as linalg::Abs_(Tensor &Tin), where Tin is the current Tensor.
the Max member function. Same as linalg::Max(const Tensor &Tin), where Tin is the current Tensor.
the Min member function. Same as linalg::Min(const Tensor &Tin), where Tin is the current Tensor.
Load current Tensor from file.

detaileddescription.para:
fname[in]
file name
load the Storage from file with file path specify with input param 'fname'
the file must be a Tensor object which is saved by cytnx::Tensor::Save.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::string &fname, const unsigned int &dtype, const cytnx_int64 &count=-1)
memberdef.name: Fromfile

briefdescription.para: Load current Tensor from the binary file.

detaileddescription.para:
This function will load the Tensor from the binary file which is saved by cytnx::Tensor::Tofile. Given the file name fname , data type dtype and number of elements count, this function will load the first count elements from the binary file fname with data type dtype.
fname[in]
the file name of the binary file.
dtype[in]
the data type of the binary file. This can be any of the type defined in cytnx::Type.
count[in]
the number of elements to be loaded from the binary file. If set to -1, all elements in the binary file will be loaded. If set to 0, this returns an empty Tensor with the requested dtype.
Tensor
The dtype cannot be Type.Void.
The dtype must be the same as the data type of the binary file.
The count cannot be larger than the number of elements in the binary file.
The file name fname must be valid.
cytnx::Tensor::Tofile

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *fname, const unsigned int &dtype, const cytnx_int64 &count=-1)
memberdef.name: Fromfile

briefdescription.para: Convert a Storage to Tensor.

simplesect.para: [Tensor] a Tensor with the same dtype and device as the input Storage

briefdescription.para: an tensor (multi-dimensional array)
